description_long: This is a sprawling log cabin constructed primarily from large, rounded timber logs with a natural wood finish, giving it a rustic and traditional appearance. The structure features a complex, multi-gabled roof covered in dark brown shingles, with several protruding sections and overhangs that add architectural interest. Multiple windows of varying sizes are distributed asymmetrically along the facades, framed in dark trim, and some are set into small dormer-like extensions. The cabin includes at least one covered porch area supported by log columns, providing a sheltered outdoor space. The overall footprint is elongated and L-shaped, with distinct wings and a central connecting section. The exterior exudes a cozy, woodland retreat vibe, suitable for residential use in a natural or suburban setting.
description: A large, L-shaped log cabin with a multi-gabled shingle roof, numerous windows, and a covered porch, all crafted in a rustic wood style.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main facade with prominent gabled roofs, multiple windows, and the central entrance area.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated structure, the depth of the roof overhangs, and the covered porch at the near end.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying additional windows, a secondary entrance, and the full L-shaped layout of the cabin.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the extended wing, porch area, and the arrangement of windows along the side.
attribute_roof_type: multi-gabled roof with intersecting gables, moderate overhangs, and dark asphalt shingles
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical distribution of multiple rectangular windows of varying sizes, some grouped and some isolated, with several set into dormer-like projections
attribute_door_placement: one main door centered on the front facade and a secondary door at the rear, both integrated into the main sections of the cabin
